meaning: adj.未得补偿的,没有得到赔偿的
examples: Training at weekends may be uncompensated.
Where this fails, the person may retain an uncompensated feeling of inferiority.
Factory workers arrive early and stay late, without pay. Training at weekends may be uncompensated.
Others complain that uncompensated internships flout labour standards, exploit nascent workers and surely depress wages for everyone else.
Competing too often results in investors taking uncompensated, unrecognized, unwanted, or misperceived risk.
